sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.32 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (E.\,Rapaport Strasser). The Lov\'asz conjecture states that a vertex-transitive connected graph is Hamiltonian. In the special case of Cayley graphs, one can ask the following. Consider a set$A$which generates a group$G$and is symmetric ($x A$implies$x^{-1} A$). Is there a list$a_1,a_2, , a_n$of elements of$A$such that$a_1, a_1a_2, a_1a_2a_3, , a_1a_2 a_n$is a complete list of the elements of$G$?...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
